content.text: Backend ES is plain HTTP on `es-http:9200`. Now I'll create a TLS cert and an Ingress that terminates TLS and rejects plain HTTP.

input.command: cd /tmp && openssl req -x509 -nodes -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout es.key -out es.crt -days 365 -subj "/CN=es.example.com" -addext "subjectAltName=DNS:es.example.com" 2>/dev/null && kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret tls es-tls --cert=es.crt --key=es.key --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - && echo OK; kubectl get ingressclass
input.description: Create TLS secret and check ingress class

description: Create TLS secret and check ingress class

summary: Create TLS secret and check ingress class

content.content:
secret/es-tls created
OK
NAME    CONTROLLER             PARAMETERS   AGE
nginx   k8s.io/ingress-nginx   <none>       2m41s
Shell cwd was reset to /mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/elasticsearch-service-and-network-repair-20260618_115905/stages/stage_04

tool_use_result.stdout:
secret/es-tls created
OK
NAME    CONTROLLER             PARAMETERS   AGE
nginx   k8s.io/ingress-nginx   <none>       2m41s

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: es-ingress
  namespace: elasticsearch
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/force-ssl-redirect: "true"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - es.example.com
      secretName: es-tls
  rules:
    - host: es.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: es-http
                port:
                  number: 9200
EOF
